element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn fuel depletion mechanic and recharge strip navigation on initial races
Parallel introduction to fuel system; player learns dual resource management

element_introduction_order.element: Upgrade purchasing (engines, gearboxes, tyres, armour, boost)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive unlock of higher-tier components across championship progression
Weapons introduced sequentially; player learns each has different strategic applications and use limits

element_introduction_order.element: Fuel starvation races (intentionally low recharge strips)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must master high-speed coasting without fuel or use nuclear engines/boost strategically

element_introduction_order.element: Track obstacles (trees, objects causing speed loss)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Collision recovery requires resource management (boost level 4+, attractor, or recharge strip proximity)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Unknown - enabled by DSP-4 chip but no specific introduction documented
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns route optimization and risk assessment for faster vs. safer paths
Four functional secret bonuses incentivize specific driver activities; discovery encourages experimentation

typical_relief_triggers: Driving over red recharge strips restores fuel and speed
Driving over blue repair strips restore frame integrity
Successful weapon use (warp to avoid crash, jump to pass car)
Reaching end of race/completing championship segment
typical_tension_triggers: Intentional low-recharge-strip track design forcing resource scarcity
Collision with obstacles causing severe speed loss
AI opponents growing progressively faster each championship race
Limited weapon uses requiring tactical deployment
Frame damage accumulation reducing safety margin
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-relief driven by fuel/repair mechanics; escalating difficulty through championship progression as AI opponents accelerate and resources become scarcer; dual-resource management creates sustained low-level tension punctuated by recharge moments

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Dual-route track branching creates optional faster paths with higher risk; DSP-4 chip enables intentionally varied route lengths allowing player route choice strategy
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Secret bonuses awarded as spherical icons scattered on track and as hidden post-race achievements for specific driver activities; encourages exploration of track edges and experimentation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Identical starting cars with forgiving recharge strip placement; player builds upgrade foundation while learning fuel/repair mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive AI acceleration; some tracks introduce fuel-starvation design; weapon system becomes available; higher-tier upgrades unlock
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced AI opponents using full acceleration; intentional scarcity of recharge strips testing mastery of resource management and high-speed coasting; access to nuclear engines and level 6 components (conditional on difficulty setting)
notable_spikes: First fuel-starvation race introduces resource scarcity tension
Mid-championship: AI growth begins outpacing early-game upgrades
Late-championship on Hard difficulty: level 6 components unavailable, forcing player to compete against faster AI with incomplete upgrade potential

notable_level_patterns.description: 20-car field (18-19 named AI opponents); player must balance fuel collection, damage avoidance, and positional advancement over race distance
notable_level_patterns.example_level: First track documented as initial championship event

notable_level_patterns.description: Intentionally designed with less than minimum recharge strips, forcing player to master high-speed coasting without fuel or leverage nuclear engine persistence and boost reserves
Single track splits into two paths with different lengths and speeds; player must assess risk vs. reward of faster route; enabled by DSP-4 chip's unique capability
